questions.question: What physical quantity is defined as the rate of change of linear momentum over time?

What is the apparent force that seems to pull a rotating object outward away from the center of its circular path when observed from a rotating frame?
Which mechanical quantity is defined as the cross product of the position vector and the applied force vector, causing rotational acceleration?
What type of collision preserves both the total linear momentum and the total kinetic energy of the system?

What is the value of terminal velocity for an object dependent upon?
answers: Air resistance and shape/surface area only
The balance point where drag force equals gravitational force
The initial height of the drop
questions.correctAnswer: The balance point where drag force equals gravitational force
